description: Next.js 15 app with TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS v4, App Router scaffolded and rendering a homepage
App shell with sidebar navigation containing links: Dashboard, Bookings, Guests, Properties, Revenue. Responsive collapse on mobile.
Dark mode toggle in header that persists preference to localStorage and applies Tailwind dark classes
JSON data files in src/data/ with typed interfaces: bookings (50 entries), guests (30 entries), properties (3 entries with names Northstar Lodge, Nyhavn Oak, Streamhouse)
Data access layer in src/lib/data.ts with functions: getBookings, getGuests, getProperties, getBookingsByDate, getBookingsByProperty, getGuestById, getRevenueByMonth
API route POST /api/bookings that adds a new booking to the data and returns the created booking
API route PATCH /api/bookings/[id]/status that updates booking status (confirmed, checked-in, checked-out, cancelled)
Dashboard homepage showing: total bookings count, total revenue, average occupancy rate, and todays check-ins/check-outs count as stat cards
Dashboard homepage shows upcoming bookings for next 7 days in a list with guest name, property, dates, and status badge
Calendar page with month view grid showing bookings as colored bars spanning check-in to check-out dates. Color-coded by property.
Calendar supports month navigation with prev/next buttons and displays month/year header
Clicking a booking on the calendar shows a detail panel with guest name, property, dates, status, notes, and revenue
Guest list page with table showing: name, email, phone, total bookings count, total revenue. Sorted by name.
Guest search input that filters the guest table by name or email in real-time as user types
Guest detail page at /guests/[id] showing guest info and a list of all their bookings with dates and status
New booking form page with fields: guest select dropdown, property select, check-in date, check-out date, nightly rate, notes. Submits to POST /api/bookings.
Booking form validates that check-out is after check-in and displays error message if invalid
Booking status badges rendered as colored pills: confirmed (blue), checked-in (green), checked-out (gray), cancelled (red)
Revenue page with monthly bar chart showing total revenue per month for the current year using recharts or chart.js
Revenue page shows breakdown by property with horizontal bars comparing revenue across the 3 properties
Revenue page shows occupancy rate percentage per property as a progress bar or donut chart
Properties list page showing each property as a card with name, address, active bookings count, and total revenue
Property detail page at /properties/[id] showing property info and a calendar view of only that propertys bookings
Loading skeleton components shown while data fetches on each page
Empty state components with helpful messages when no data exists (no bookings, no guests found, etc.)
Toast notification component that appears on successful booking creation or status update
Global search bar in the header that searches across bookings (by guest name) and guests (by name or email) with dropdown results
All pages render correctly on mobile viewport (< 768px) with sidebar collapsed to hamburger menu
Unit tests for data access layer functions (getBookings, getGuests, getRevenueByMonth) using vitest
All tests pass with npm run test and build succeeds with npm run build
